reviews.review_description: This is not what Sherlock Holmes would've wanted... I'm given a list of objects to find in a room - which are all out in the open - and I point and click on them. That is the extent of the game. Am I missing something? Unfortunately, didn't get to the game until after the refund date.

My first impression was that it's like playing a smartphone game in VR.  

I visited the developer's website and it looks like this is their first VR game. Their previous games are smartphone hidden object games, which is essentially what this VR game is, so my initial impression was accurate.

It's a Quest 3 exclusive but the graphics look dated. There's no real time lighting. The skybox in the first outdoor location looks like something from the early days of 3D rendering as the stars distort into lines as they approach the horizon. The windows have blurry opaque glass so you can't get a look at the street from interiors.

The animations are primitive, and sometimes absent.  The first character I met moves very robotically and "slide walks" and takes turns mechanically. By "slide walk" I mean the character glides smoothly along moving their legs but the feet have no connection to the floor, they just swish back and forth with no respect to the walking speed.  Opening a door causes it to simply vanish, there's no animation of it swinging open.

The controls are primarily point & click with teleportation movement.

The game plays in a rigid semi-interactive manner. Looking for clues pins you to one spot until you've found them all, just like a smartphone hidden object game.

The puzzles I encountered are ridiculously simple and pretty much solved themselves.

Young children who enjoy hidden object smartphone games would probably have a good time with this. However their parents might object to some of the content, (including references to smoking), so I have no clue who the intended audience is, but it's certainly not me, so I'm requesting a refund and moving on.

Im enjoying this game because its so reminiscent of the hidden object games I used to love to play on the pc. The puzzles however are very repetitive and not very challenging.  The environments are very immersive and cool.  One thing Ive noticed is that Sherlock's voice keeps changing  different AI voices which is very noticeable.  Its the first hidden object game to come to the Quest and definitely could be improved upon but enjoyable. Its a great price right now so snatch it up if you like HO games.

I liked Sherlock Holmes: The Master Detective mainly because of its atmosphere and detective vibe. Solving the cases feels interesting, and the game actually makes you think. Some parts feel a bit outdated and slow by today’s standards, but overall it’s still a fun game if you enjoy mystery stories.

I cant move at the front door, the instrustion ask me press X/A to call the menu but no response. I am stucked there forever.

I found that I can't call out the menu by pressing X/A. What's wrong? I'm using Quest3 with Quest Pro controller.

I really enjoyed playing this game! The atmosphere is incredibly immersive, and the hidden object mechanics feel fresh and engaging in VR. The environments are beautifully detailed, and each case keeps you curious and thinking. Definitely worth trying if you like puzzle and detective games 👍

One more nail in the coffin. 
I got the absurdly and intensely anachronistic Sherlock Holmes VR. 
Come to think of it, the game isn't as anachronistic as Apocalypto let alone Jurassic Park. 
In Apocalypto, according to the making of documentary, tribes that lived hundreds of years and thousands of miles apart in some cases were all lumped together in a public square as if they were contemporaries of one another. 
With Jurassic Park, dinosaurs of the Triassic such as the stegosaurus, the Jurassic, and the Cretaceous period when Tyrannosaurus lived were all lumped together. Each of these periods is 100 million years long! 
So with this Sherlock Holmes VR, I'm getting off lightly. 
I have been a fan of Sherlock Holmes for over 30 years. 
"The game's afoot."
"The game is not worth the candle."
"I speak of my hopes and not of my expectations."
"When you have eliminated the impossible, whatever remains, however improbable, must be the truth."
"That would be neither useful nor necessary." 
You get quite a bit for the price. Not too simple, not too difficult puzzles. Just right. 
This looks like a random scene. Not canon and not Sherlock Holmes in-Universe with the Sir Arthur Conan Doyle novels. 
You never see Sherlock Holmes. You never see the Persian slipper where Holmes keeps his tobacco hanging at the fireplace. 
I never felt that I was in cobblestone gas lit Victoria alley ways of Sherlock Holmes stories. The developer probably never read any Sherlock Holmes novels. 
Graphics, not bad. Better than average. Immersive. 
June's Journey type find-it puzzles. 
This game is made for someone who is in grade five. However, people of all ages can enjoy it. 
I got it on sale for $10 CAD. 
More intense authenticity to the original Victorian atmospherics should do it. AI with only the dimmest grasp of Sherlock Holmes made this app. Typical of the very new media of VR. It's reach exceeds its grasp. 
"The media is the message." Marshall McLuhan

Being able to pick up and inspect almost every object makes the investigations feel much more immersive than a typical hidden object game.
Walking around the scenes and searching for clues actually makes you feel involved in the investigation.
The mix of hidden object gameplay and detective mechanics works well.
It still needs a bit more polish and content, but the foundation is very promising. Honestly had more fun than I expected.

The puzzles are interesting and make you think, and the interactive elements work well in VR. Small details like sounds and simple interactions add to the atmosphere. Not perfect, but overall a nice experience.

Sherlock Holmes The Master Detective is a decent detective game, but it’s not without its flaws. The investigation concept and clue-finding are interesting, but the game can sometimes feel slow and a bit confusing. You often have to read a lot and draw your own conclusions, so it’s not for everyone. The detective atmosphere is done well, though at times the mechanics feel a bit outdated. Overall, it’s a solid game for people who enjoy slow, logic-based investigations, but it definitely won’t appeal to everyone.

The game lived up to my expectations. It’s something completely new compared to what I’ve played before. The atmosphere of London and the Sherlock era creates a sense of space and mystery, and the characters around you feel real. The missions and the game environment are so captivating that you want to play through every level without taking a break. I’m eagerly awaiting a sequel or something new.
Great game, especially the soundtrack. You can feel the atmosphere of Sherlock, mysteries and adventures. There should be more games like this. Thanks to the developers!

reviews.review_title: exactly what I wanted in an object searc
reviews.review_description:
This ticks all the boxes for me. 

In essence, There are two primary points of gameplay. The first is situational or environmental - you move around outside, within a building, or around a room to discover things. The second is more of a static hidden object scene. However, you do get to lean forward and have some three dimensionality.

This is exactly what I was looking for. I don't want to spend coins because it times out on me; and I don't think it's fair when objects are actually hidden behind things you cannot physically see so you have to pay for a hint. There are a couple of goals, such as completing a screen in 30 seconds. ￼This game is pretty soothing overall. I don't feel babied but I really enjoy it. The plot is pretty good, you can tell they put a lot of time in the energy into the art. So just to be honest, I'm almost 50 years old. I used to love highlights magazine. If I am playing a hidden object game on iOS or via Google Play or android/Kindle and I get stuck, I usually wind up trying to cast it to my TV. My eyes aren't great, and my kids do a much more efficient job than I at these sorts of things. Anyhow, having this full-sized is super cool. ￼￼￼

Some of the animations could be further refined when the people speak, but frankly it does the job. I like the voice acting overall! ￼

I'm extremely happy to support this game and developer and would be more than delighted to be considered as a beta tester, not that any such thing exists right now. It is May 2026. 

If you enjoy playing anything like ￼Scavenger hunt by pop core games, cruise director, or "find it scavenger hunt hidden object puzzle game"￼￼I think you will love this game.

I really hope that additional content will be developed. There is zero out there right now for the Meta three in any capacity that is like this. 
￼
Great game with a really immersive atmosphere. The puzzles are interesting and keep you hooked from the start. Smooth gameplay and nice presentation.

The game is engaging and very atmospheric. I really enjoy the mechanics of searching for clues and exploring detailed locations in VR. The visual style fits the Sherlock Holmes theme perfectly. Highly recommended for puzzle fans!

reviews.review_title: Great detective experience with a few qu
reviews.review_description: The game does a great job of making you feel like a real investigator, piecing together clues and solving cases. Some parts can be a bit unclear, and occasionally I found myself unsure of what to do next, but that also adds to the challenge.
Nice game, but short and primitive. Good for kids

reviews.review_title: I liked this game a lot!
reviews.review_description:
Even though it’s not an ultra-hardcore detective simulator, it does a great job of making you feel like you’re really Sherlock Holmes in VR. Investigating crime scenes and carefully examining objects is immersive and atmospheric.
The VR mechanics make exploration genuinely interesting and enjoyable.
I also liked that the game stays accessible for a wide range of players. It focuses more on immersion, story, and atmosphere rather than overly complicated mechanics, and for me that was the right decision, because I don’t enjoy when puzzles become too difficult while you’re already getting tired from wearing the headset.

reviews.review_title: I’d love to see longer investigations
reviews.review_description:
You can really tell the developers put effort into the atmosphere and immersion. 
The game feels like a strong foundation for a great VR detective series. 
I finished the first case in one sitting and genuinely enjoyed the investigation mechanics. 
Would love to see longer and more complex cases in future updates.

I enjoyed the game “Sherlock Holmes: Master of Deduction” because of its atmosphere and detective-like vibe. Solving the cases was engaging and made me think. Some parts feel a bit slow and tedious, so there’s definitely room for improvement, but overall, it’s still a wonderful experience for fans of detective stories.

reviews.review_title: A surprisingly nice detective VR game
reviews.review_description:
The game really makes you feel like an investigator, searching for clues and connecting pieces of the story together.
Exploring the scenes in VR adds a nice level of immersion, and the detective mechanics are enjoyable overall.
The soundtrack and environments create a great mysterious atmosphere.

If you love crime-solving and hidden object games, this is a must-play. 
Investigating clues and looking for evidence feels amazing in VR. 
The puzzles are fine, the mechanics are decent, and overall the gameplay is pleasant.

The graphics and audio create a wonderful sense of presence. It’s a very comfortable rating experience, didn't feel dizzy once. Being able to play at my own pace without a clock ticking down makes it a perfect game to unwind with.

reviews.review_title: Really good game with great potential!
reviews.review_description: This is exactly what I was looking for! The atmosphere is amazing. The gameplay is straightforward but very engaging. Looking forward to seeing how the devs expand on this project. Definitely worth a try.

Loved playing this game! My only wish is that it lasted longer, but it was still totally worth it. 
I really hope there will be more cases added in the near future. Will there be future updates?
Good atmosphere and nice music. Gameplay is pretty fun too.

One of the most breathtaking sherlock stories i’ve ever heard before. Therefore, you now take part in this mysterious world. The crimescenes gameplay is pretty good, you’ll have to pay attention to pass it.

reviews.review_title: Not the Puzzle Game I Hoped For
reviews.review_description:
When I bought this game, I thought it would be a regular puzzle game like The Room or The 7th Guest, where you solve codes and mechanisms. I didn’t realize it was actually a “find all items” type of game. Unfortunately, I waited more than two weeks before trying it, so I couldn’t refund it. Personally, I’m not a fan of hidden-object games — they strain my eyes, feel repetitive, and start to feel more like work than play.

Also, there are only two chapters so far, which feels very short for the price. That said, if you don’t mind searching for items, what’s here is still decently made. So take my review as a reference: if “find all the items” games aren’t your thing, this one might not be for you.

reviews.review_title: One of the best detective games!
reviews.review_description: Genuinely the most immersed I've felt in VR since forever. You're not clicking on objects — you're actually searching a crime scene. The environments are insane for an early access title, and the branching dialogue with suspects gives you that real detective buzz. Picked it up on a whim for under €7 and it's already one of my favourite Quest games, full stop. If you like mystery or puzzles even a little, just buy it.
I enjoyed solving puzzles and searching for clues.

reviews.review_title: Good game but needs locomotion asap
reviews.review_description: Fantastic game but seriously needs locomotion,  its unfortunately a point and click teleport only movement which Im not a fan of at all and normally refund a game once I find this out but the story and nice graphics makes me wsnt to keep it in spite of the dissapointing no location.

I've played this past the house scene and into the estate and then I set it down. It has the potential to be great! It absolutely needs to add locomotion and smooth turn as options though. You can teleport pretty much anywhere, so doesn't seem like it'd be that hard to update. Snap turn and teleport should be an option, but not the only option.

Also need to work on the audio. The background sounds good, but all the voice files are blown out. I turned the volume way down but still sounds the same. The actors sound pretty good though!

Other than that, Im just getting started, but it looks like it'll prove to be a good game. Until I'm not limited to teleport and snap though, it doesn't work for me, so currently 3 stars.

OK thisis early access and considering that, its not bad. Graphics are ok and the teleport locomotion doesnt bother me. The character voices (specifically Sherlock's) need sorting because there are at least three different voices/accents and it's very distracting! Much more variety is required in the puzzles because simply standing there and pointing at random objects (hamburgers, owls, toy planes, etc) gets very repititious and boring after a while. I like the tool box feature. Need to be able to collect items and store them on your person. It's a good start and I look forward to seeing how this game progresses and (hopefully) improves.

I was really enjoying this game. I got through the 1st 2 levels over the course of a week or so, playing a little every other day. It was nice that you could start where you left off ...until one day and I had finished level 2 and was ready to play the 3rd and last case. I was so disappointed that my game progress was gone completely and I had to start over. Well, I'm not going to do that. I'll just end up deleting the game because that was a LOT of work to get to that point. Especially with all of the "seek and find" puzzles. 
That was a huge disappointment. 
That's why I rated it with 3 stars. Otherwise, I would give it a 5.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing and for sharing your thoughts! We’re really glad you enjoyed the game.

Just to clarify — the current Early Access version includes two full cases so far. The next chapter will arrive in one of the upcoming updates.

We truly appreciate your time and feedback — it helps us improve before the full release! Stay tuned for future updates with more cases.

reviews.review_title: Nice puzzles, bad acting and plot logic
reviews.review_description:
The game so far (Early Access, only 2 chapters available at this time) is a combination of Hidden Object Game, some puzzles, and a bit of story. At the end of a chapter, you need to determine who you want to accuse of having committed the crime (I don't know yet if there is a consequence for choosing poorly).

So far so good. The puzzles are easy to medium difficulty, and the hidden objects are a relaxing and enjoyable thing to do in VR.

The game feels a bit like it was meant for a teenage audience, and the difficulty level seems to support that.

There are numerous references to the Cumberbatch version of Sherlock Holmes in the game (posters, scattered pictures) - too many, actually. It feels a bit uncomfortable and forced after a while as if they were trying to get you to associate their product with the high quality of the TV show - unsuccessfully, though.

The voice acting is humorously terrible at times. I have counted at least three different dialects that Sherlock himself speaks - upper class English most of the time, then some Cockney, and then even some American English - all in the same chapter. I believe there are at least two or maybe three different voice actors involved for this one character.

The other characters are voiced very woodenly and without life. Some recordings were done in a studio-like environment, others sound like they were done in someone's office.

Then you have the logic errors in the story. Sherlock goes between suspecting a character and trusting them at the same time - unthinkable for the actual character. One particular "plot twist" is way too foreseeable.

And while the supernatural aspect of the game is kinda fun outside of this context, I can't see how they could possibly make it work with such a rational character as Sherlock.

In the end, the game is enjoyable enough, and I'll continue playing it when more content comes out. But I cannot take it seriously at this time.

I’m really enjoying this! I never thought I’d get into adventure hidden-object games, but in VR it finally clicks. Point-and-pick just works: aim, grab with one button - no pixel hunting or guesswork. It feels direct and very satisfying.
What I love most is how easy it is to play and truly relax: warm lighting, great mysterious atmosphere, subtle sound, and rooms full of believable little objects you can pick up and inspect. The hint system is gentle- there if you want it. Nothing super hard, but consistently interesting - exactly what I want in the evening!
Best of all, the story is genuinely engaging—proper Sherlock intrigue with layered clues that tie your finds together. A cozy, clever mystery I’ll definitely keep coming back to. Thanks!
Great game about Sherlock. Totally recommend it
